name: SPINAL FIXATION DEVICES
chapter: DIFFERENTIAL DIAGNOSIS OF SKULL AND SPINE DISORDERS
definition: screws threaded into vertebral body over staples embedded into vertebral body
dominant_finding: connected by plates / rods spanning single / multiple segments
distribution: 2 radiopaque markers for assessment of spacer position: posterior marker should be ≥
discriminating_features: connected by plates / rods spanning single / multiple segments
2 radiopaque markers for assessment of spacer position: posterior marker should be ≥
corticated linear lucency across graft material

general: connected by plates / rods spanning single / multiple segments
2 radiopaque markers for assessment of spacer position: posterior marker should be ≥
corticated linear lucency across graft material
increased tracer activity on bone scintigraphy
lucent rim around screw threads ← loosening
penetration into adjacent disk space / foramen transversarium / spinal cord / nerve
< 3° of intersegmental positional change on lateral flexion + extension views
visible bone formation in / about graft material
absence of fracture of device / graft / vertebra
